name: Cowboy Pintos
recipeIngredient: 2 stalks celery, cut into 1/2 inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Sort and wash beans. Soak overnight in 2 inches of water, covered. Drain and set aside.
Add fresh cilantro, parsley, and/or basil at the end.
2. Heat oil in a large, heavy pot over medium-low heat. Cook onion for 10 minutes or until wilted. Stir occasionally.
3. Add garlic during last 2 minutes of cooking. Add carrots, celery, and drained beans to the pot.
4. Stir in tomatoes, reserved tomato juices, water, thyme, oregano, and cilantro sprigs. Bring mixture to a boil, then reduce heat to medium. Simmer, uncovered, for about 1 1/2 hours or until beans are tender but not mushy. Skim any foam from the surface.
5. Remove tied cilantro and add freshly chopped cilantro; season with pepper to taste. Ensure beans are softened before adding salt.
6. Serve over cooked rice or barley.
